description_long: This object is a robust, rectangular obelisk constructed from dark gray concrete, featuring a broad, sloped base that tapers slightly as it rises. The front and back faces are adorned with a bold, diagonal black-and-white striped band running horizontally across the center, likely serving as a high-visibility warning or demarcation. The surface texture is matte and slightly rough, with subtle variations in shading and minor surface imperfections typical of cast concrete. The base is solid and wide, providing stability, and includes three rectangular cutouts along the bottom edge, possibly for handling or placement. The top of the obelisk is flat and unadorned, with no visible tip ornamentation or inscriptions. This structure is commonly found in urban environments as a traffic barrier, boundary marker, or protective amenity.
description: A sturdy, gray concrete obelisk with a wide base and a central black-and-white striped band, featuring a flat top and three cutouts at the bottom.
description_view_0: The obelisk is seen from the narrow end, showing the sloped sides and a dark rectangular patch on the base.
description_view_1: The front face displays the full width, with a prominent diagonal black-and-white striped band and three rectangular cutouts at the bottom.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow end is visible, mirroring view 0 with a similar sloped profile and dark patch.
description_view_3: The back face is shown, identical to the front with the same striped band and cutouts.
attribute_base_design: The base is wide and rectangular with sloped sides, featuring three evenly spaced rectangular cutouts along the bottom edge. The design is utilitarian, with no decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and unadorned, matching the width of the upper section with no unique features or decorations.
